rubric_results.requirement: Module dependency DAG is acyclic and correctly directed
rubric_results.evidence: Verified all import statements across core modules. models.py imports only stdlib (uuid, dataclasses, datetime, enum, typing). executors.py imports from models + llm_client only (lines 18-26). engine.py imports from models, executors, events, hooks, store — never from server or agent (lines 18-75). store.py imports only from models (lines 11-21). agent.py imports from executors + models only — never from engine or server (lines 26-33). server.py sits at the top, correctly importing from engine, agent, models, store, events, hooks, config (lines 21-41). No circular imports. DAG is strict: models → llm_client → executors → engine → server.
rubric_results.file: src/stepwise/models.py:1-16, executors.py:18-26, engine.py:18-75, store.py:1-21, agent.py:26-33, server.py:21-41

rubric_results.requirement: Error handling is consistent — no bare except clauses, errors logged and propagated
rubric_results.evidence: No bare `except:` clauses found (good). However, 25 instances of `except Exception: pass` silently swallow errors. High-severity: engine.py:1735 (legacy tick loop drops all exceptions silently), models.py:896 (validation exit condition errors silently ignored), server.py:1499 (corrupted JSON templates silently skipped with no logging). Two overly broad patterns: server.py:1902 and server.py:2683 use `except (YAMLLoadError, Exception):` which catches all exceptions, not just YAML parse errors. ~15 low-severity instances in cleanup paths (executor cancellation on job cancel, shutdown handlers) are defensible best-effort patterns but still lack even debug-level logging. The critical-path swallowing in engine tick, validation, and template loading are the main concern for 1.0.

rubric_results.requirement: All dataclasses have complete to_dict()/from_dict() pairs
rubric_results.evidence: All 22 @dataclass definitions in models.py have both to_dict() and from_dict() methods: ChainConfig, StepLimits, DecoratorRef, ExecutorRef, ExitRule, InputBinding, ForEachSpec, OutputFieldSpec, ConfigVar, FlowRequirement, CacheConfig, StepDefinition, FlowMetadata, WorkflowDefinition, Sidecar, HandoffEnvelope, WatchSpec, SubJobDefinition, StepRun, JobConfig, Job, Event. Implementations use .get() with sensible defaults, isoformat()/fromisoformat() for datetimes, and proper enum handling. Store layer consistently uses from_dict() for hydration and to_dict() for persistence.

rubric_results.requirement: Tests cover happy path, error cases, and edge cases
rubric_results.evidence: 1,942 tests across 88 files. Engine tests are deep behavioral tests: test_transitive_currentness (57 lines, verifies currency invalidation across DAG when A loops), test_exit_rule_eval_error_falls_through (tests error recovery in exit rule expressions), test_fan_out_fan_in (multi-step data synthesis with artifact validation). YAML loader includes 6 security tests blocking AST injection (test_class_traversal_blocked, test_dunder_globals, test_lambda_blocked, test_fstring_blocked). Model tests verify round-trip serialization for all 22 dataclasses. Dedicated edge case files: test_optional_inputs, test_for_each, test_concurrency, test_poll_executor, test_delegation, test_session_continuity. Weakness: 80% happy path skew with only 5% explicit edge case tests, but critical paths (engine readiness, currency, loops, expression safety) are well covered.

rubric_results.requirement: User inputs validated at system boundaries (API, CLI, YAML)
rubric_results.evidence: API: All POST endpoints use Pydantic BaseModel (server.py:116-151). Path traversal prevention via resolve()/relative_to() (server.py:2054-2088). File extension whitelist (server.py:2145-2151) and size limits (server.py:2154-2155). Label name regex validation (server.py:1581). SQL: All queries use parameterized ? placeholders — no string interpolation (store.py:206, 241, 260-362). YAML: Root structure validated (yaml_loader.py:1187-1193), identifier regex for input names (yaml_loader.py:215-218), AST validator blocks dunder attributes, f-strings, lambdas in expressions (yaml_loader.py:84-95). Shell: shlex.quote() applied to inputs (engine.py:120), env var name blocklist rejects LD_PRELOAD etc. (executors.py:330-333), simple commands use shlex.split() without shell (executors.py:356). Note: adversarial probes found shell injection when flow authors embed $var in double-quoted bash strings (shlex.quote's single quotes ineffective inside DQ) — this is a security architecture issue tracked separately, not a missing boundary validation.

rubric_results.requirement: Database connections, file handles, and subprocesses properly cleaned up
rubric_results.evidence: Database: SQLiteStore.close() at store.py:854-855; server lifespan (server.py:689-788) cancels all async tasks in order then calls store.close() at line 787; runner.py:587 closes store in finally block. Subprocesses: agent.py:538-548 uses os.waitpid() for reaping; agent.py:579-621 implements cooperative ACP cancellation then SIGTERM fallback; hooks.py:114-138 uses communicate() with timeout and proc.kill(). Files: consistent context managers (server.py:264,296); agent.py:524-525 properly closes parent FDs after Popen; tempfile cleanup in hooks.py:144-148 and cli_llm_client.py:66-70 (shutil.rmtree in finally). AsyncEngine.shutdown() (engine.py:3036-3042) shuts down thread pool. WebSocket dead connections pruned in _broadcast(). Minor gap: runner.py:558 creates StepResultCache without closing in finally block — low impact for short-lived CLI process.

qualitative_summary: Code quality is strong for a pre-1.0 project. Module boundaries are clean with strict DAG enforcement — no circular imports across 6 core modules. All 22 model dataclasses have complete serialization pairs. Input validation is thorough at critical boundaries: Pydantic for API, parameterized SQL, AST-safe expression evaluation, and path traversal protection. Resource cleanup is systematic with proper context managers, subprocess reaping, and orchestrated server shutdown. Test coverage is deep where it matters most — engine readiness/currency/loops, security (AST injection prevention), and complex DAG scenarios — with 1,942 tests across 88 files. The primary weakness is error handling consistency: 25 instances of `except Exception: pass` silently swallow errors, including in critical paths (legacy tick loop engine.py:1735, validation logic models.py:896, template loading server.py:1499) and overly broad exception catches in server.py. These should be addressed before 1.0 — add logging to high-severity instances and narrow exception types. The two blocker-severity shell injection findings from adversarial probing are a separate security concern warranting its own remediation track.
